sections.title: Why is Africa Unique?
points: Cradleland of Man: Believed to be the origin of mankind.
Archaeological Proof: The skull of early man (Zinjanthropus) was discovered at Olduvai Gorge (Tanzania) by Dr. LSB Leakey.
Dark Continent: A historical name used by Europeans because they had very little knowledge of Africa's interior for a long time.
Central Position: It is the only continent crossed by the Equator, the Tropic of Cancer, and the Tropic of Capricorn.

sections.title: The People: Africa's Pygmies
points: Shortest People: Africa is home to the Pygmies, the shortest group of people in the world.
Habitat: They primarily live in the Ituri Forest located in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC).
Way of Life: Traditionally, they were hunters and gatherers living in harmony with the thick tropical rainforest.

sections.title: Wealth Under the Ground: Minerals
points: Africa is a leading producer of high-value minerals in the world.
Key Minerals: The continent produces large quantities of Gold and Diamonds.
Other Resources: It is also rich in Copper (Zambia), Oil/Petroleum (Nigeria/Libya), and Platinum.

sections.title: Record-Breaking Water Features
points: Lake Victoria: The largest lake in Africa and the 2nd largest freshwater lake in the world.
Lake Volta: Located in Ghana, it is the world's largest man-made lake.
River Nile: The longest river in the world (6,670 km), starting in Uganda and ending in the Mediterranean Sea.
Victoria Falls: The largest waterfalls in Africa, found on the Zambezi River on the border of Zambia and Zimbabwe.
Suez Canal: A strategic man-made waterway in Egypt (162 km long) connecting the Red Sea to the Mediterranean.

sections.title: Extreme Points of Africa
points: Highest Mountain: Mt. Kilimanjaro in Tanzania (5,895 meters).
Hottest Point: The Sahara Desert, which is also the largest hot desert in the world (8.4 million km²).
Southernmost Point: Cape Agulhas in South Africa.

sections.text: Fact Check: Ethiopia is the most populated landlocked country in Africa, while Seychelles is the smallest country on the continent.

cards.fact: The specific home of the Pygmies in the Democratic Republic of Congo.
The site in Tanzania where Dr. Leakey found the Zinjanthropus skull.
The largest falls in Africa, located on River Zambezi.
The largest man-made lake in the world, found in Ghana.
cards.mnemonic: Volta sounds like Voltage (Electricity from a man-made dam).
